errors.message: Metadata Error: AxLicenseCode/ConDemoIsvSuite/PublicKey: Duplicate value '2' detected.
errors.note: PublicKey is globally unique across every installed model; slot 2 is owned by ApplicationFoundation/AxLicenseCode/LogisticsBasic.xml. Re-running the identical chain with PublicKey=700 (a free slot) builds clean in 12s with 0 errors.

root_cause_hypothesis: get_knowledge(topic="license-codes") teaches the AxLicenseCode shape with a literal PublicKey of 2 and never states that PublicKey is a globally unique ISV key slot enforced by xppc across all installed models. The case instruction was authored from that same knowledge and therefore hard-codes the colliding value, so a perfectly grounded agent is steered straight into 'Metadata Error: Duplicate value 2 detected'. Secondary: no query tool surfaces the set of used PublicKey slots, and validate_code(references) resolves 0 references in AxLicenseCode/AxConfigurationKey XML, so neither the knowledge layer nor the static gate can catch the collision before the build. The create/serialisation path itself is correct - the emitted XML is byte-identical in shape to ApplicationPlatform/AxLicenseCode/Asset.xml, the <LicenseCode> and <ParentKey> links are emitted, and the chain compiles with 0 BP warnings once the slot is free.
suggested_fix_area: src/knowledge (license-codes topic: state PublicKey global uniqueness, drop the literal 2 from the example, point at the free-slot check); eval/cases/L2-license-code-configkey.json (stop mandating PublicKey=2); src/validation reference resolver (teach it AxLicenseCode/AxConfigurationKey refs: Label, LicenseCode, ParentKey, PublicKey collision); src/eval/oracle/cli.ts (--classification is written through unvalidated, accepting values outside the schema enum)
evidence_refs: xppc: Metadata Error: AxLicenseCode/ConDemoIsvSuite/PublicKey: Duplicate value '2' detected.
